title: Digital printing may revolutionize the packaging industry
contentHtml:
Australian packaging giant Pact Group has installed a new digital printing platform it says will revolutionize plastic packaging decoration in Australia and New Zealand.
The technology is licensed from Polytype Converting AG of Friborg, Switzerland. Melbourne-based Pact Group Holdings Ltd has exclusive rights to the technology in Australia and New Zealand.
Mark Nothnagel, general manager of technology at Pact Group, told Plastics News that this process of printing directly on the container is more efficient and flexible than traditional printing and in-mold labeling (IML) methods, eliminating the need to separate printing plates and colors and reducing color variations.
The $1.2 million digital technology platform prints images and designs directly onto polypropylene, polystyrene and polyethylene packaging, reducing turnaround time.
The company is currently conducting quality trials on PET products.
Nothnagel said: "This decoration technology will help customers quickly change packaging designs and seize one-time promotional opportunities. Direct digital printing is a new generation of decoration technology. It will not only bring customers flexible and better branding opportunities, but will also greatly shorten the supply chain and streamline the manufacturing process."
Digital technology marks the beginning of a major shift in branding of plastic products. Benefits include high-definition, lifelike images; the ability to decorate the entire container; fast response times; and the ability to print small runs or individual packages.
"Traditional printing methods take anywhere from 1 to 3 weeks to set up before you start production, depending on the method you use," Nothnagel said. "If you want to change the design, you have to start the entire process all over again. Digital printing takes less than an hour to print the design onto the container and start producing the finished product. It opens up a wide range of possibilities for businesses that use plastic containers."
Pact Group has commercialized the technology on a small scale and is trialling it with several companies in Australia and New Zealand.
Digital printing does not replace other printing methods such as IML, offset printing or DuoSmart (plastic containers with a paper outer layer).
It will become an alternative process that provides a more flexible option for manufacturers requiring small batches or targeted packaging.
Digital technology can personalize packaging "instantly," Nothnagel said.
"We anticipate that this technology will allow customers to respond very quickly to seasonal or location-based promotions, online marketing campaigns and sponsorships," he said.
Pact Group is to launch a second digital printing platform specifically for bottle caps. The printing press is currently being commissioned and will be put into use in mid-2015.
titleZh: 数字印刷或将掀起包装界革命
contentHtmlZh:
澳大利亚包装巨头Pact集团已安装了一个新的数字印刷平台，据称将在澳大利亚和新西兰的塑料包装装饰领域掀起革命。
该技术由瑞士Fribourg的Polytype Converting AG公司授予许可。位于墨尔本的Pact集团控股有限公司在澳大利亚和新西兰享有这项技术的独家代理权。
　　Pact集团的技术总经理Mark Nothnagel告诉《塑料新闻》说，这种直接在容器上打印的工艺比传统的印刷和模内贴标（IML）方法更有效率，更灵活，不必分离印版和色彩，且减少了色差。
　　这个耗资120万美元的数字技术平台可把图片和设计直接打印到聚丙烯、聚苯乙烯和聚乙烯包装上，缩短了周转时间。
该公司目前正在对PET产品进行质量试验。
　　Nothnagel表示："这项装饰技术将帮助客户快速改变包装设计，把握一次性的促销机会。直接数字印刷是新一代的装饰技术。不仅将为客户带来灵活且更好的品牌塑造机会，还将大大缩短供应链，精简制造工艺。"
数字技术标志着塑料产品的品牌塑造开始重大转变。其优点包括高清晰度、逼真的图片；可以装饰整个容器；响应时间快；且能够印刷小批量或单个包装。
　　Nothnagel说："传统印刷方法在开工前需要1-3周的时间进行设置，具体取决于你所用的方法。如果你要改变设计方案，就必须将整个过程从头来过。而数字印刷只需不到一小时就能把设计印刷到容器上并开始生产成品。它为使用塑料容器的企业开启了广泛的可能性。"
　　Pact集团已将该技术小规模商用化，且在澳大利亚和新西兰的多家公司进行了试用。
　　数字印刷并不会取代其他印刷方法，如IML，平版印刷或DuoSmart（带有纸质外层的塑料容器）。
　　它将成为一种替代工艺，为需要小批量或针对性包装的生产企业提供更加灵活的选择。
　　Nothnagel说，数字技术可"立即"赋予包装以个性化特色。
　　他说："我们预计该技术将让客户得以非常快速地响应季节性或基于不同地点的促销、在线营销活动和赞助活动。"
　　Pact集团将推出第二种专门用于瓶盖的数字印刷平台。这台印刷机目前正在调试中，将在2015年中期投入使用。
